summary: End-to-end testing of KYNTHIA iteration_2: removed emergent badge, trimmed Manifesto from 4 to 2 stats, added 6 client-routed bespoke showcase pages, rewrote Hero into magazine-style editorial grid with TorusGeometry. Backend 11/11 pytest pass. All 7 frontend routes render without errors, all data-testids resolve, Manifesto shows exactly 2 stats, emergent badge anchor is absent, 6 portfolio items navigate correctly via react-router, each showcase has tier badge + back-to-kynthia pill that returns to /, quote form e2e creates persisted DB record. 0 console / page errors.

minor.issue: Returns 200 instead of 201 for resource creation. Cosmetic — already noted in iteration_1.

issues: Long all-black/white sections between hero and footer on showcase routes (intentional editorial whitespace), but combined with bespoke per-page art direction it can feel sparse on first scroll. Cosmetic only.
TorusGeometry ring + sparse particles look elegant on dark bg. THREE.Clock deprecation warning still present (carryover from iteration_1) — non-blocking.

critical_code_review_comments: All 6 showcase pages share identical structure but live as 6 separate ~150-line files. Consider extracting a <ShowcaseLayout tier={...} backTo='/' /> component to DRY up Back pill + tier badge + masthead. Not blocking.

Showcase headline text is split across multiple
per line for stagger animation — semantic concern: an h1 broken into 3 sibling divs. SEO/accessibility-wise an

line1line2

with sr-only continuous label could be cleaner. Cosmetic.

index.html still loads https://assets.emergent.sh/scripts/emergent-main.js — this is the script that *would* inject the badge; current CSS safety-net + removed <a> in source means no badge renders. If emergent-main.js ever re-injects via JS at runtime, the CSS rule (a[href*='emergent.sh'] { display:none !important }) catches it. Verified: 0 anchors found at runtime.

Portfolio uses <Link> from react-router-dom inside framer-motion <motion.div> — works correctly, no scroll-restoration issue observed (each showcase mounts at top because BrowserRouter doesn't auto-scroll, but pages are short enough this isn't visible).
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Created 1 TEST_pw_1777356384 quote via Playwright + several TEST_-prefixed quotes via pytest. All persisted in MongoDB.quotes; safe to leave or purge.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All critical features from review_request verified. Quirks: (1) showcase headlines are split across <div> per typographic line — use textContent + check for *parts* of the headline, not the full sentence. (2) Use case-insensitive matching since some hero text is rendered lowercase but spec referenced uppercase. (3) For full-text presence, use document.body.textContent (not Playwright inner_text) because framer-motion whileInView elements have opacity:0 until scroll-in. (4) Lenis intercepts native scroll — use window.__lenis.scrollTo(el, { immediate: true }) before clicking off-screen elements. (5) Public preview /api routes enforce User-Agent — urllib.request gets 403, use curl with -A or requests with default UA when validating from python.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. iteration_2 changes (emergent badge removal, manifesto 2-stat, magazine hero, 6 showcase routes) are all correctly implemented and rendering. Initial false-negative test results on showcase text searches were due to my own test script using inner_text() which drops opacity:0 framer-motion content + searching for full sentences when text is split across animated <div>s — fixed by using textContent and substring/parts matching.
